Japanese: 同日　午後　１時２１分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: January 8, 1:21 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: ‥‥いよいよ出てくるね、 ゼニトラ。
English: So we're finally going to see The Tiger on the stand!

Japanese: ここまで来たら、 あとヒトイキだよ！
English: We've almost got this case won now, Nick.

Japanese: だといいけど‥‥
English: I wish I could agree.
Huh?

Japanese: 本土坊さんの、さっきの尋問‥‥
English: When I cross-examined Mr. Armstrong just now...

Japanese: たしかに“ゼニトラに命令された” ‥‥そう証言したけど‥‥
English: ...he said he was just doing what The Tiger told him to do.

Japanese: ゴドー検事も、言ってただろ？
English: But Godot picked up on it, remember?

Japanese: 証言が《真実》だったかどうか？ ‥‥証拠はないんだよ。
English: He pointed out that without proof, we don't know if what he testified is the truth.

Japanese: 本土坊さんがウソを ついてた、ってコト？
English: You mean, you think Mr. Armstrong was lying?

Japanese: 検察側が、そう主張してきたら ヤッカイなことになるだろうね。
English: I don't know. But if that's the line the prosecution takes, we could be in trouble.

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥決定的な証拠が 足りない気がする‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling that we don't have the case-making evidence we're going to need.)

Japanese: コラァッ！　アンタ！
English: Hey, pal!

Japanese: あ‥‥イトノコさん‥‥
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: なに、コーフンしてるんですか？ アタマ、逆立てちゃって。
English: What are you so jumpy about, Detective!? Your hair's standing on end!

Japanese: アンタこそ、ノンキにチョンマゲ 結ってる場合じゃないッス！
English: Hey, that's the pot calling the kettle black, little Ms. Topknot!

Japanese: チョンマゲって言うな！
English: It's not a topknot!

Japanese: と‥‥とにかく、落ちついて くださいよ、イトノコ刑事！
English: Never mind about the hair! Just calm down, alright?

Japanese: ううう‥‥ミョーに時間があいて、 じっとしてられないッス！
English: I-I-I can't stand still when I don't have a job to do! I-I-I kinda get wound up.

Japanese: うほほおおおおおおおおおおおい！
English: Aaaaaaargh!

Japanese: （うるさいな‥‥）
English: (No kidding!)

Japanese: ‥‥ナニか！ ナニか、シゴトはないッスかッ！
English: You gotta have something you need me to do, pal! Anything!
Well, um...

Japanese: ちょっと、署まで ひとっ走り、行ってくるッス！
English: Hey, I'm gonna take a jog back down to the precinct.

Japanese: １時間もあれば、指紋の 検査ぐらい、できるッスよ！
English: I could get some prints analyzed for you if you've got an hour!

Japanese: （‥‥“１時間”って、審理が 再開しちまうぞ‥‥！）
English: An hour!? The trial will have reconvened by then!

Japanese: でも。でも。ないんでしょ？ “決定的な証拠”が！
English: But, Nick! We still don't have a really decisive piece of evidence, right?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥切りフダがなきゃ、 戦えないな‥‥）
English: (True. Without some kind of trump card to pull out of the bag, we're really stuck...)

Japanese: ‥‥１時間あれば、 “指紋”の検査ができるんですね？
English: You said you could get some fingerprint analysis done in an hour?

Japanese: そッス！
English: You bet!

Japanese: それなら‥‥この証拠品の 指紋、詳しく調べてもらえますか？
English: In that case, would you mind checking the prints on this for me?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってよ なるほどくん！
English: What are you doing, Nick!?

Japanese: せっかく指紋を調べるんなら、 こっちにしようよ！
English: If Detective Gumshoe's going to check some prints for us, shouldn't he be checking this?

Japanese: それは‥‥今朝、自分が 持ってきた小ビン、ッスな。
English: That's that small bottle I gave back to you this morning, right?

Japanese: キッチンで拾った、 岡　高夫の耳のクスリ‥‥か。
English: The victim's ear medication that we found in the kitchen?

Japanese: 《正体不明の指紋が付着》って 法廷記録に書いてあったし。
English: Look, it even says, "covered in unidentified fingerprints" right in the Court Record!

Japanese: そッスね！　ジツは 自分も気になっていたッス！
English: You're right! Actually, that's been gnawing at me, too.

Japanese: いっちょ、たのみますよ！ イトノコさん。
English: So you'll look into it for us? Awesome!

Japanese: あの。ぼくが見せた証拠品は‥‥？
English: Um, what about the thing I asked you to run a check on?

Japanese: ああ。そっちも、ヒマがあったら 調べてみるッス！
English: Oh, that. Sure, I'll see what I can do if I've got any spare time.

Japanese: （ひ、ヒマがあったら‥‥）
English: (Spare time...? I feel so unimportant right now...)

Japanese: じゃ、せっかくですから、 コイツの指紋を調べてください。
English: If you're going back to the station anyway, could you find out whose prints are on this?

Japanese: それは‥‥今朝、自分が 持ってきた小ビン、ッスな。
English: Oh, hey. That's the small bottle I gave back to you this morning, right?

Japanese: 証拠品の中で“正体不明”なのは、 この指紋だけですからね。
English: Yeah. I think it's time we solved the last mystery of who the prints on it belong to.

Japanese: そッスね！　ジツは 自分も気になっていたッス！
English: Sure thing, pal! Actually, that's been gnawing at me, too!

Japanese: 証拠品《小ビン》を イトノコ刑事にあずけた。
English: Small Bottle given to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: じゃッ！　自分はさっそく、 調査に行くッス！
English: OK, I'll get this off to the lab right away.

Japanese: 自分が戻るまで、 負けちゃダメッスよ！
English: Just make sure you don't lose the case before I get back!

Japanese: （‥‥勝負は、 いよいよ大詰めだな‥‥）
English: (This is pretty much the final showdown, I guess...)

Japanese: どっちがホンモノか‥‥ 決着をつけてやろうね！
English: It's time to separate the phonies from the real guys!

Japanese: 同日　午後　１時５６分 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: January 8, 1:56 PM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: それでは、審理を再開します。 ゴドー検事。芝九蔵さんは‥‥？
English: Court will now reconvene. Mr. Godot. Did you find this Furio Tigre...?

Japanese: ‥‥捕獲してきた。コーヒー３杯の 安いシゴト‥‥だったぜ。
English: I even tamed him for you. It was a 3 cup job. No problem.

Japanese: （ほ、ホカク‥‥？）
English: (T-Tamed him...? The guy's name may be Furio Tigre, but come on...)

Japanese: ‥‥やっこさん、そうとう 盛り上がってるからな。
English: He's pretty lively.

Japanese: 噛まれないよう、注意するコトだ。
English: Be careful... He still bites.

Japanese: それでは、芝九蔵　虎ノ助さんを 入廷させてください！
English: Very well. Please show Mr. Tigre to the stand!

Japanese: ‥‥ええと、それでは証人。 名前と、職業を‥‥
English: ...Um... Witness. Please state your name and occupation for th--

Japanese: おらあああぁぁぁぁぁぁぁぁぁッ！
English: Grrraaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: きゃきゃわわわはああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、テーブルの下に かくれちまったぞ‥‥！）
English: (Don't hide under the table Maya! ...Unless there is room for me down there too!)

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。よよよよよ、 よろしいでしょうか‥‥
English: I, uh... Um... W-W-W-Would you mind...?

Japanese: ンなんじゃいィィィィィッ！
English: What'youse say to me!?

Japanese: ンなんでもないですゥゥゥゥゥッ！
English: N-N-N-Nothing! I didn't say nuddin'! Honest!

Japanese: （なんだそりゃ！）
English: (Who could've guessed that fear would induce a bad Brooklyn accent in the judge?)

Japanese: ワイはこれから、ダイジな 取り引きに出るトコロやったんや！
English: I got business to take care of, ya hear me?

Japanese: ダレやァッ！　いったいダレが、 ワイを呼び出したんやぁぁっ！
English: So who the hell called me into dis hole?

Japanese: ‥‥キサマかッ！
English: Was it you', spikey!?

Japanese: え、ええと、それは‥‥ もちろん、裁判長‥‥
English: Ack, no. Of course not... It was... the judge...

Japanese: さ、裁判長ッ！
English: Y-Your Honor!?

Japanese: あ！　ちょっとボールペンを 落としてしまってですね‥‥
English: Oh, dear! I, um... I seem to have dropped my pen. Where on earth is it...?

Japanese: 私にかまわず、みなさんで やってください！
English: Don't mind me! Just carry on with the proceedings as normal.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (That's it. We're doomed.)

Japanese: ダレやァッ！　いったいダレが、 ワイを呼び出したんやぁぁっ！
English: Maybe youse didn't hear me! I said, who the hell was it dat called me in here!?

Japanese: ‥‥いくら叫んでもムダだぜ。
English: There's no need to shout. We can all hear you.

Japanese: なんやてェ‥‥？
English: Whad'youse say?

Japanese: アンタは、とらわれたのさ。 法廷という、オリの中に。
English: There's no point struggling. You're caught in a snare. The relentless snare of the law!

Japanese: 裁くのは、このオレだぜ！
English: And I'm the one that hauled you in!
Grrr...

Japanese: （か‥‥カッコイイ‥‥）
English: (T... Too cool...)

Japanese: なるほどくん！　負けちゃダメ！
English: Don't let him get the better of you, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥最初にカクニンしておくぜ。
English: Let's start with the basics.

Japanese: アンタ‥‥この事件について、 知ってるコトはあるかい？
English: You know about the incident in question, correct?

Japanese: ハッ！　なんのことかサッパリや！
English: Incident!? I don't know nuthin' about no stinkin' incident, mask boy!

Japanese: 須々木　マコの前回の法廷に、 傍聴人として来たことは？
English: You mean you didn't attend the previous trial of Maggey Byrde?

Japanese: あらへんワ！　裁判見物するほど、 ヒマやないッ！
English: Maggey who!? I've got more important things to do dan watch courtroom dramas!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。では、 証言をおねがいできますかな。
English: Of course. Well, perhaps you could give us your testimony, then?

Japanese: 事件当日のコトについて。
English: Please tell us about what you did on the day of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥フン！ 成歩堂　龍一ィ‥‥
English: ...Hmph. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: おおかた、キサマの サシガネなんやろう？
English: Youse de one who set dis up, didn't you'?

Japanese: ‥‥一生かかっても後悔しきれへん ようにしたるでェ‥‥
English: Youse gonna regret the day you ruffled The Tiger's fur. You' hear what I'm sayin'?

Japanese: うううう‥‥ （‥‥やっぱり、恐ェ！）
English: *gulp* (Maybe I should've brought a diaper with me today...)

Japanese: なるほどくん！　しっかり！
English: Get a grip, Nick!

Japanese: ～事件当日のアリバイ～
English: -- The Tiger's Alibi --

Japanese: 殺人事件なんて言われても、 ワイはなんにも知らんでェ。
English: I don't know nothin' about no murder.

Japanese: 去年の１２月は忙しくてなあ。 ずっと事務所におったからのぉ。
English: I was tied up with business in December last year. Spent all'a my time in my office.

Japanese: カリヨーゼには毎日、カネを求めて シャチョーさんが行列を作るんや！
English: I got whales lined up to borrow cash from Tender Lender every single day.

Japanese: ‥‥ワイのアリバイなら、 うらみちゃんが証明してくれるで！
English: Youse wanna check my alibi, just ask Violetta.

Japanese: いやいや。やっとボールペンが 見つかりました。
English: Ah, at last I found my pen.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします
English: Very well then, Mr. Wright. Your cross-examination, plea--

Japanese: うらああァァァァァァァァッ！
English: Aaaaaargh!

Japanese: ななな、なんですか！ 急に大きな声、出さないように！
English: Wh-What is it!? Please, witness, if you could refrain from shouting out like that.

Japanese: ‥‥そこのニイちゃんのコトは、 よぉく知ってるでェ‥‥。
English: I know de kinda games dat guy in the blue plays!

Japanese: ちっちゃなコトをチクチクつつく ことしかできない、ヨゴれ弁護士！
English: That lowlife ain't no lawyer! He just punches away at stupid details 'til he wins.

Japanese: よ、ヨゴれ‥‥
English: L-Lowlife...? Me?

Japanese: ええかァ。事件に関係ないコトを 聞こうとしたら‥‥
English: Listen up, smarty! Every time you' ask me some'ding dat doesn't relate to dis case...

Japanese: １回、５００万円！ ウチから借りてもらうでェ‥‥
English: ...I'm gonna bill you 50,000 dollars! And youse gonna borrow the cash from me!

Japanese: え‥‥。 （借金の押し売りかよ！）
English: Uh... (That's one loan contract I refuse to sign!)

Japanese: ‥‥ヒリつくような、タマの 取りあい、しようやないかィ！
English: And don't think it ain't gonna hurt when you' get tangled with The Tiger!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　おもしれえぜ。 ‥‥見ているぶんには。
English: Ha...! I love a good spectator sport.

Japanese: ちょちょちょちょっと！ さすがにそれは‥‥
English: J-J-Just a minute! That's really not...

Japanese: ‥‥この証人はなぁ。
English: This witness is...

Japanese: いわば、都会のジャングルに 放たれた、ハラペコのトラ。
English: How can I put it? A hungry tiger roaming the urban jungle!

Japanese: 逆らうと‥‥そのジマンのアタマ、 噛まれちゃうぜ！
English: Get on his bad side, and he'll bite everyone's heads off. Yours, too!

Japanese: しかたないですな。私のほうから、 ペナルティを与えましょう。
English: Very well. I have no choice but to impose a penalty system here.

Japanese: ‥‥ワイはこれから、ダイジな 取り引きがあるんや。
English: Youse better be listening. I said I got business to take care of. Big business!

Japanese: そろそろ、バスに とび乗る時間やでェ‥‥
English: If I don't split now, I ain't gonna catch my bus...

Japanese: 事件に関係のない《ゆさぶり》には ペナルティを与えます。
English: The court will impose a penalty for any irrelevant pressing of witness testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥そのつもりで、 尋問をおねがいしましょう。
English: Keep that in mind as you begin your cross-examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: わ‥‥わかりました。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: がんばれ！　なるほどくん！
English: You can do it, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥いいかげん、出てこいよ。
English: Come out from under there already, would you, Maya?

Japanese: ～事件当日のアリバイ～
English: -- The Tiger's Alibi --

Japanese: 殺人事件なんて言われても、 ワイはなんにも知らんでェ。
English: I don't know nothin' about no murder.

Japanese: 去年の１２月は忙しくてなあ。 ずっと事務所におったからのぉ。
English: I was tied up with business in December last year. Spent all'a my time in my office.

Japanese: カリヨーゼには毎日、カネを求めて シャチョーさんが行列を作るんや！
English: I got whales lined up to borrow cash from Tender Lender every single day.

Japanese: ‥‥ワイのアリバイなら、 うらみちゃんが証明してくれるで！
English: Youse wanna check my alibi, just ask Violetta.

Japanese: 関係ないところをゆさぶると、 もれなく５００万円もらえるんだ！
English: Just think! All you have to do is press him on something irrelevant, and bam! $50,000!

Japanese: ば、バカ言え！　おこづかいで 返さなくちゃいけないんだぞ。
English: Get real, Maya! How about you pay back the money then. Your allowance should cover it!

Japanese: ナニよそれ！ バカにしてるよ！
English: Hey! I'm no idiot, you know!

Japanese: （５００万円もらうつもりでいる ほうが、バカにしてるぞ‥‥）
English: (Well, I'M certainly not the one who thought the $50,000 was free money!)

Japanese: ‥‥できれば、ゆさぶらずに スパッと決めたいな。
English: Anyway, I need to work this guy without pressing his testimony too hard.

Japanese: でも‥‥ムジュンなんて あるかなあ、この証言‥‥。
English: But his testimony is pretty solid as it is...

Japanese: （たしかに、アキラカな ムジュンは見あたらない）
English: (I admit nothing jumps out at me as a contradiction.)

Japanese: （とにかく、攻撃の突破口を 作るしかないな‥‥！）
English: (But I've got to find an opening somewhere... Or I need to make one.)

Japanese: 事件当日もトーゼン、事務所や。 そんなワカゾウ、見たコトないワ！
English: The day youse talkin' about, I was in de office, too. I never saw that kid before.

Japanese: 吐麗美庵の事件を知らない‥‥ そんなハズはありません！
English: I don't buy that you haven't even heard of the incident that occurred at Trés Bien!

Japanese: ほっほお‥‥オモロイこと、 言うてくれるなァ‥‥。
English: Heh heh. Youse got a big mouth, Wright!

Japanese: ワイが、そのケチな事件に 関係しとる、言うつもりなら‥‥
English: Youse tryin' to say I was in on some mob hit? Is dat what you' tryin' to say?

Japanese: ショーコ見せんかいコラァ！
English: Where's your proof, then!?

Japanese: しょ‥‥証拠‥‥ですか。
English: Um... E-Evidence, yes... Well...

Japanese: 《法廷で発言力を持つのは 証拠だけ‥‥》
English: "For an attorney to make an accusation in court, he must have evidence."

Japanese: 『入門シリーズ・弁護士になろう』 に、そう書いてあったでェ！
English: That's from "Loan Shark to Lawyer: A Beginner's Guide", yeah?

Japanese: お！　証人。なかなか ベンキョウ家ですなあ。
English: Ah! I see you're the studious type, Mr. Tigre. Very good.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　弁護をたのんだら どうだ？　まるほどう‥‥
English: Ha...! Why don't you ask him to represent you sometime, Trite?

Japanese: （くやしいな‥‥どうする？）
English: (Grrr... What should I do?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人ッ！
English: Mr. Tigre!

Japanese: なんじゃいィィィィッ！
English: Whad'youse want!?

Japanese: あの‥‥。よろしかったら、 もう少し詳しく証言を‥‥
English: Err... If you wouldn't mind going into a bit more detail...

Japanese: ‥‥無用なゆさぶりは不要‥‥ ヤクソクだぜ、まるほどう！
English: This is a dead end, Trite, and you know it. Remember the rules!

Japanese: 証拠を持たないゆさぶりは 禁じているはずです。
English: Pressing the witness without evidence to back up your attack is prohibited.

Japanese: ‥‥ヤクソクどおり、 ペナルティを与えましょう。
English: As I said earlier, the court will impose a penalty for such behavior!

Japanese: （やりにくいな‥‥）
English: (Ouch. The pain...)

Japanese: （‥‥先の見えないゆさぶりは キケンだ‥‥）
English: (It's too dangerous to press things without knowing where they might end up.)

Japanese: （今は、他の情報を見つけた ほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (I'd better just focus on gathering information at the moment...)

Japanese: それでは、証人。
English: Now then, Mr. Tigre...

Japanese: もう一度、最初から証言を おねがいできますかな？
English: Would you mind repeating your testimony from the beginning, please?

Japanese: ワイの時間は高いんや。 わかっとるやろうなァ‥‥
English: My time ain't free, you' hear? Youse betta remember dat!

Japanese: バスに間に合わなかったら、 セキニン取ってもらうでェ‥‥
English: If I miss that bus, someone's gonna pay!

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: １ヶ月前のコトですよ。 まちがいありませんか？
English: Are you sure about that? We're talking about one month ago, you know.

Japanese: ウチにはなァ。 スゴウデの秘書がいるんや。
English: You' see these teeth!? That's how sharp my secretary is.

Japanese: スゴウデ‥‥ （うらみさんのコトか‥‥）
English: Sharp...? (Is he talking about Viola Cadaverini?)

Japanese: ワイのスケジュール帳に、 しっかり書かれとったワ！
English: She writes everything in my scheduler.

Japanese: 《１２月・おおむね事務所》 ‥‥ってなァ！
English: "December: Mainly in the office." That's what it says, so that's where I was!

Japanese: それは、なんというか‥‥ 大ざっぱ、なのでは‥‥
English: That seems like a rather, err... sketchy schedule...

Japanese: ‥‥ギヌロ！
English: Gwwrrr...

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (There he goes again! Hmm... What The Tiger did all December isn't the issue...)

Japanese: （‥‥問題は“事件が起こった日” なんだけど‥‥どうする？）
English: (What's important is what he was doing on the day of the murder... So, now what?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人ッ！
English: Mr. Tigre!

Japanese: なんじゃいィィィィッ！
English: Whad'youse want!?

Japanese: あの‥‥。よろしかったら、 もう少し詳しく証言を‥‥
English: Err... If you wouldn't mind going into a bit more detail...

Japanese: ‥‥無用なゆさぶりは不要‥‥ ヤクソクだぜ、まるほどう！
English: This is a dead end, Trite, and you know it. Remember the rules!

Japanese: しかし！　事件当日のアリバイは、 ハッキリさせるべきです！
English: No! It's essential that we establish the witness's alibi accurately.

Japanese: ‥‥私もそう思いますよ。
English: ...I agree.

Japanese: 被害者が殺害された、１２月３日。 この日も、事務所に‥‥？
English: The victim was killed on December 3rd. Were you in the office that day, too?

Japanese: アタリマエやッ！ 一歩も出てないでェ。
English: Maybe youse ain't listening. Of course I was! I never set foot outside.

Japanese: 事務所で、シャチョーさんの 相手をしとったんや。
English: I had meetin's all day with a bunch of cats wantin' to do business wid me.

Japanese: そんなワカゾウ、知らんでェ！
English: I ain't never seen that young kid before!

Japanese: ‥‥今の証言は、 さすがに重要ですな。
English: ...I do believe the witness's last statement was important.

Japanese: あの‥‥ゴドー検事。 おねがいします‥‥
English: Um... Mr. Godot, if you could please...

Japanese: 証人。‥‥今の発言を 証言につけ加えてくれ。
English: Mr. Tigre. The court asks you to add that last statement to your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥フン！
English: ...Hmph!

Japanese: （自分で言えよ、裁判長！）
English: (Don't let an animal beat you! Be a man, Your Honor, and ask him yourself!)

Japanese: そんなに客が来る ものなんですか？　闇金融って。
English: So I take it you get a lot of customers at your black market loan office?

Japanese: 今日び、小さな会社は 経営が苦しいからのお。
English: It's tough for de little guys these days. Small businesses are feelin' the pinch.

Japanese: ワイは、そんなシャチョーたちの あたたかい味方なんや。
English: Bosses of companies like that wanna get in good with me. I'm deir best friend.

Japanese: ほうほう。 それはカンシンですなあ。
English: I see. That's very admirable of you, witness.

Japanese: 《三日坊主》‥‥それが、 カリヨーゼのモットーやからな！
English: Yeah, well, Tender Lender's motto is "Win Through Compromise" ya know.

Japanese: みみ、三日坊主‥‥？ それは、どういうイミですかな？
English: "Win Through Compromise"? What is that supposed to mean?

Japanese: “つかまえた客は、３日で 丸坊主にムシる”というコトや。
English: "Compromise the customer to win." That's what it means. Youse got it?

Japanese: （‥‥どこが “シャチョーの味方”だよ！）
English: (...With "best friends" like you, who needs enemies!?)

Japanese: ‥‥つまりッ！
English: Here, let me spell it out for youse all!

Japanese: そのニイちゃんのギザギザ アタマも、あと３日したら‥‥
English: You' don't pay me back within 3 days, Spikey, I take what's due, and you' stupid hair!

Japanese: ああなる、言うコトやッ！
English: Youse follow what I'm saying!?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼく、お客じゃ ないんですけど‥‥。
English: Um... But I'm not one of your customers...

Japanese: （カリヨーゼのこと、 もっと聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (Should I ask more about Tender Lender or not?)

Japanese: “うらみちゃん”というのは、 あの《鹿羽組》の‥‥
English: By "Violetta", you're talking about the Cadaverini family's...?

Japanese: せやで。コワいでェ、 あそこの組長さんは‥‥
English: Yeah. You' don't wanna cross them, or youse gonna wind up sleepin' with de fishes.

Japanese: アンタがうらみちゃんを 疑ってると知ったら‥‥
English: Once the Godfather finds out youse been draggin' Violetta through the mud...

Japanese: 組長の‥‥‥に‥‥‥されるでェ。
English: ...he'll... do you... in...

Japanese: あの‥‥語尾がよく 聞こえなかったんですけど。
English: Sorry, I... I couldn't quite put it all together towards the end there...

Japanese: ちょっと“うらみさん風” だったね、今の。
English: He said that just like Viola, didn't he?

Japanese: （今は、彼女のことを聞く ときではない気がするけど‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling this might not be the right time to probe him about her...)

Japanese: 岡　高夫を知らない‥‥ 本当ですか？
English: You didn't know Mr. Glen Elg? Really?

Japanese: ワイの取り引き相手は シャチョーさんだけや。
English: I don't mess with the guppies when there are bigger fish to fry.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタもどうや、 シャチョー！
English: Whaddya say, boss!?

Japanese: え！　わ、私ですか！
English: Hmm? Who? Me!?

Japanese: ぽぉんと２０００万ぐらい、 つまんでおかへん？
English: Howse about I hit you with a fat 200,000 dollars?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。たしかに。 裁判長ともなれば、外車のひとつも
English: Hmm... Well, I was thinking about purchasing a nice car from Europe...

Japanese: （考えこんでるよ‥‥）
English: (He's seriously considering this!?)

Japanese: ショーバイ上手だねえ、 ゼニトラさん。
English: Wow, The Tiger's a really good salesman, huh?

Japanese: こっちも、ショーバイ上手で 行かないとね。
English: Yeah. And if I want to sell this case, I need to copy him!

Japanese: ぽぉんと証拠品の１つも、 たたきつけておかへん？
English: How about you hit him with a fat piece of evidence, boss?

Japanese: （そんなカンタンに 行けばいいけど‥‥）
English: (I wish it was that easy...)

Japanese: ゼニトラさん‥‥
English: Mr. Tigre...

Japanese: あなたは“知らない”と 言っていますが‥‥
English: You claim you didn't know Mr. Glen Elg.

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんのほうは、 あなたを知っていた。
English: But it appears that Mr. Elg knew you.

Japanese: なんやてェ‥‥
English: What!?

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんのカレンダーに、 メモが残っていたんです。
English: Mr. Elg left this little note on his calendar.

Japanese: 《ゼニトラに会う》‥‥ 日付は、１２月３日です。
English: "Meet with The Tiger"... Dated, December 3rd.

Japanese: じゅ、１２月３日‥‥
English: D-December 3rd!?

Japanese: まさに‥‥ 事件当日ではないですかッ！
English: That's... the day of the murder!

Japanese: ゼニトラさん！　あなたが 岡　高夫を知らないハズがない！
English: So, Mr. Tigre, I submit that you did indeed know one Mr. Glen Elg!

Japanese: このとおり‥‥２人は 事件当日、会っているのですから！
English: Because on the very day of the incident, you met with him!

Japanese: ぐわあーッはッはッはッはッはァ！
English: Grrrah ha ha ha hah!

Japanese: ‥‥合格や、ニイちゃん！
English: Not bad! Youse actually not bad.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: S-Sorry?

Japanese: アンタを試してみたんや。 ‥‥なかなかやるのォ。
English: I was just messin' with youse to see how good you' were.

Japanese: なかなかやるって。なるほどくん。
English: Did you hear that, Nick? He said you're not bad!

Japanese: （ウソつけ！）
English: (That's one compliment I can do without! Plus, he's lying through his teeth!)

Japanese: ‥‥あの、証人。 ウソはいけないと思うのですが‥‥
English: Um, witness. Please remember that you're under oath. Lies will not be tolerated.

Japanese: ウソなんかついとらへんでェ！
English: Youse callin' me a liar!? Is that what you' doin'!?

Japanese: ひひいぃぃっ！
English: Ruh-roh!

Japanese: “岡　高夫に会ったことがない” ‥‥その証言は、ホントだ、と？
English: So you're saying that your claim to have "never seen that kid before" is the truth?

Japanese: アタリマエやッ！
English: I said I'm dead serious! Youse betta believe that's the truth!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
どうやら‥‥コーヒーをもう１杯。 ‥‥楽しめそうだぜ。
English: Then I'd say that gives me time... to enjoy another cup of pure black magic!

Japanese: アンタが、もう一度 証言しているあいだにな‥‥。
English: That is, while you testify for the court again, Mr. Tigre.

Japanese: あ。では、証人。なにとぞ、 証言をよろしくおねがいします。
English: Oh, yes! Um, would you mind indulging the court, witness?

Japanese: （“被害者に会ったことがない” ‥‥ウソに決まっている！）
English: (He never actually met the victim!? That's got to be a lie right there!)

Japanese: （一気に追いつめてやる‥‥！）
English: (It's time I nailed this guy!)

Japanese: ～被害者・岡　高夫について～
English: -- The Victim, Glen Elg --

Japanese: ワイはウソはつかへん。 岡　高夫に会ったことはないで！
English: I ain't no liar. I never met Glen Elg.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥そんな名前のオトコに 融資の相談は受けたけどなぁ。
English: There was some lame guy with that name, though. Wanted to borrow cash from me.

Japanese: 会うヤクソクはあった。ただし‥‥ その場所は《カリヨーゼ》やで。
English: I set up a meetin' with the guy... at my office, Tender Lender.

Japanese: 事務所で待っとったんやけど、 ケッキョク、現れなかったんや。
English: I waited around for him, but he ain't ever showed.

Japanese: ワイは、行ったことないでェ。 《吐麗美庵》とかいうレストラン。
English: I ain't never even been to that Trés Bien joint, youse hear?

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。 スジは通りますな。
English: I see... That all seems perfectly logical.

Japanese: ヤクソクはしたが、 事務所に現れなかった。
English: You had arranged to meet with the victim, but he didn't show up.

Japanese: トーゼン、だろうぜ。 ‥‥死んじまったワケだからな。
English: I've heard it's pretty hard to keep appointments when you're dead.

Japanese: それでは、成歩堂くん。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well. You may begin your cross-examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい！
English: Yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥ワイは今日、 デカい取り引きがあるんや。
English: Didn't I tell you I got a big deal goin' down today?

Japanese: もう、バスじゃ間に合わへん。 急行に飛び乗らなアカン。
English: I ain't gonna make my bus now. I'm gonna have to take the express train.

Japanese: 急行代‥‥アンタに払って もらうでェ‥‥ニイちゃん！
English: That bill's goin' straight to you... Wright!

Japanese: うううう‥‥。
English: Grr...

Japanese: ～被害者・岡　高夫について～
English: -- The Victim, Glen Elg --

Japanese: ワイはウソはつかへん。 岡　高夫に会ったことはないで！
English: I ain't no liar. I never met Glen Elg.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥そんな名前のオトコに 融資の相談は受けたけどなぁ。
English: There was some lame guy with that name, though. Wanted to borrow cash from me.

Japanese: 会うヤクソクはあった。ただし‥‥ その場所は《カリヨーゼ》やで。
English: I set up a meetin' with the guy... at my office, Tender Lender.

Japanese: 事務所で待っとったんやけど、 ケッキョク、現れなかったんや。
English: I waited around for him, but he ain't ever showed.

Japanese: ワイは、行ったことないでェ。 《吐麗美庵》とかいうレストラン。
English: I ain't never even been to that Trés Bien joint, youse hear?

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんと会ったコト ないのかなあ。ゼニトラ‥‥。
English: Do you think he really didn't meet Glen Elg?

Japanese: そんなハズはないよ。
English: Not likely.

Japanese: この事件は‥‥アイツが 仕組んだことなんだからね。
English: Because I'm convinced this guy's behind the whole thing.

Japanese: じゃあ、今の証言って、 やっぱり‥‥ウソ？
English: So you mean... that whole testimony he just gave was a lie?

Japanese: モチロンさ。アイツに 考えるヒマを与えないように‥‥
English: Of course. So we can't afford to give him time to think of ways to plug the leaks in it.

Japanese: どんどん攻めていこう！
English: I've got to nail him while I still can!

Japanese: しかし！　岡　高夫さんの カレンダーに、あなたの名前が‥‥
English: And yet, your name was written on the victim's calendar!

Japanese: ウマのカレンダーがなんじゃい！ こっちは《トラ》やでェ！
English: Get that horse calendar outta my face! I'm The Tiger, not a lame horse, you' porcupine!

Japanese: そんなウマなんぞ‥‥ このトラが食ったらァァッ！
English: Tigers like me eat horses like that for breakfast!

Japanese: いやいや！　証拠品を食べる 証人がありますか！
English: No, no, no! Witnesses aren't permitted to eat evidence. Not that they ever would!

Japanese: いや。そういえばムカシ、 １人だけ、いたような‥‥
English: Well, there's was a case once, where a young lad ate--

Japanese: とと、とにかく！
English: A-Anyway!

Japanese: 被害者に会ったことがないのなら、 このメモの説明をおねがいします！
English: If you had never met the victim before, how do explain this note he left?

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいですね。 彼の会社の社長の話では‥‥
English: That's very strange. According to the manager of the firm where the victim worked...

Japanese: １２月３日は、《返済の期限日》 ‥‥つまり。
English: ...December 3rd was the date he had to repay his loan.

Japanese: 《借りる》相談ではなくて 《返す》話をするはずだった‥‥
English: He wasn't due to discuss borrowing money, but repaying what he owed...

Japanese: あ‥‥ああ。 そうやったかもしれんなぁ。
English: Oh. Yeah. Maybe youse right.

Japanese: まあ、なんにせよ、たしかに １２月のはじめのコトやったワ。
English: Whatever it was, it was goin' down at the beginnin' of December.

Japanese: それじゃあ‥‥ 岡　高夫に会ったワケですね！
English: So you did meet Mr. Elg, then!?

Japanese: ‥‥あわてちゃアカンで、 ニイちゃんや‥‥。
English: Hey, Wright. Youse might wanna cool your jets a sec.

Japanese: ‥‥《吐麗美庵》じゃ なかったんですか？
English: So your meeting wasn't supposed to be at Trés Bien, then?

Japanese: なんで、そんなレストラン 行かなアカンねん。
English: Why would I wanna go to a dump like dat?

Japanese: 話があれば、ウチの事務所で すればエエ。
English: If I wanna talk with my clients, I got an office decked out for the job.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴツいサンドバッグもあるし。
English: I got the best punching bag youse ever saw in there.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ行きたくないな‥‥）
English: (Not exactly a professional office you've got there, you know...)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥事件当日は、 事務所にいたわけですか？
English: So you were in your office on the day in question?

Japanese: 現れなかった‥‥？
English: He didn't show up?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　トーゼン、だぜ。
English: Ha...! What do you think, Trite?

Japanese: 現れたくても‥‥死んでた ワケだからな。レストランで。
English: Let's see. How does a dead man get from a French restaurant to a loan shark's office?

Japanese: あなた自身は、吐麗美庵に 行かなかったんですか？
English: And you yourself didn't go to Trés Bien?

Japanese: ほほお。このゼニトラに アヤをつける気ィか‥‥？
English: What are you' tryin' to pin on me here?
Um...

Japanese: 今度、ウチの事務所で ハラぁ割って語り合おかァ。
English: Why don't youse come down to my office? We can chat about this thing my way, yeah?

Japanese: ‥‥ゴツいサンドバッグもあるし。
English: I got the best punching bag youse ever saw in there.

Japanese: い、いえいえ！ エンリョしておきます！
English: No, no! No need! I'm good, thanks!

Japanese: ええかァ！　これだけは 言うとくでェ‥‥。
English: Listen up! I ain't got nothin' more to say, except this.

Japanese: 行ったことがない‥‥ 一度も、ですか？
English: Never? Not even once?

Japanese: せや。トーゼン、事件当日も 事務所にいたで。
English: Not even once. I was in my office the day that went down.

Japanese: ワカゾウのコーヒーに、毒なんぞ 入れられるワケ、ないのォ。
English: I didn't put no poison in no kid's coffee. Capice?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 芝九蔵さん。 ‥‥このマッチはなんですか？
English: Mr. Tigre. Is there something you'd like to tell the court about these matches?

Japanese: マッチ‥‥やて？
English: Matches? What youse talkin' about?

Japanese: 《カリヨーゼ》で見つけました。 あのレストランの紙マッチです。
English: We found them in your office at Tender Lender. They're from "that" restaurant.

Japanese: 《吐麗美庵》に行ったことが ないのならば‥‥
English: If you've really never been to Trés Bien before...

Japanese: どうして、あの店のマッチが あなたのデスクにあったのですか！
English: ...what was a book of the restaurant's matches doing on your desk!?

Japanese: ダマらんかいィッ！ 店のマッチ、店のマッチって‥‥
English: Youse been snoopin' around in my stuff now too, wiseguy!?

Japanese: テメェはワイの新妻かいィッ！
English: What are you', my ball and chain!? Ain't no broad controlling me!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ いかがでしょう？　証人。
English: Order! Order! Well, witness?

Japanese: そろそろ、ホントのコトを ワレワレにぶつけてみては‥‥
English: I think it's time you started telling us the truth, don't you?

Japanese: うらああァァァァァァァァッ！
English: Grrraaaaargh!

Japanese: ご‥‥ごめんなさいごめんなさい！
English: S-Sorry! I'm terribly sorry! Forgive me!

Japanese: オトコは、一度言うたコトは そうカンタンに曲げへんでェ‥‥。
English: I ain't no pussy-cat! I don't go back on what I said!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ワイはあの日、 レストランに行った！
English: But, OK... I was at the joint that day.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Whaaat!?

Japanese: だがなァ、ニイちゃん‥‥
English: But listen good, alright?

Japanese: それでもワイは、その ワカゾウには会ってないんや！
English: I might've been there, but I still never met that kid.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Well, well...

Japanese: もう一度、おしゃべりを したほうがいいみたいだぜ‥‥。
English: Looks like an order just came in for another testimony.

Japanese: （あと、一歩‥‥ なんとしても、認めさせる！）
English: (I'm this close to proving it was him!)

Japanese: （アイツは、あの日！ 岡　高夫に会って、そして‥‥）
English: (He did meet Glen Elg that day.)

Japanese: （毒を入れたんだ！ 被害者のコーヒーに‥‥）
English: (And he did put poison in his coffee! He must have!)

Japanese: ～事件当日‥‥吐麗美庵～
English: -- At Trés Bien --

Japanese: あの日の午後、‥‥レストランで 会うことになっていたんや。
English: I was supposed to meet wid the kid at the restaurant that afternoon.

Japanese: 入り口のドアを開けた瞬間‥‥ イヤなものが見えた。
English: When I opened the door to the joint, I saw one ugly scene.

Japanese: あのワカゾウ、机につっぷして ピクとも動かへんやないか！
English: The guy was laid out over the table, stiff as concrete.

Japanese: こりゃヤバい‥‥ピンと来た。 その場で回れ右、や。
English: I figured if the place wasn't hot already, it was gonna be, so I split.

Japanese: パトカーのサイレンも聞こえたし。 そのまま事務所に帰ったワケや。
English: I heard the cop's sirens on my way out, and I went straight back to my office.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。ケッキョク、 岡　高夫さんとは会っていない‥‥
English: I see... You didn't actually meet with him in the end, then.

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。尋問を。
English: Well, Mr. Wright. Your cross- examination, please.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: アカン！　そろそろ特急に 乗らなアカン時間や！
English: Hold it! If I wait around here any longer, I ain't even gonna make the normal express.

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、ムダな質問は ユルされへんでェ‥‥
English: No more stupid questions.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　問題、ねえぜ。
English: Ha...! No problem.

Japanese: 必要のない《ゆさぶり》には、 オレからペナルティを与えてやる。
English: Any time Trite presses you on something irrelevant, I'll see he pays a penalty.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！　それは 私のシゴトですから‥‥
English: M-Mr. Godot! That's my job!

Japanese: アンタのシゴトは、木槌を叩いて 《有罪！》と叫ぶコトだけ、だぜ！
English: Your job is to slam that little hammer of yours and call a guilty verdict.

Japanese: キチンとシゴトしなッ！
English: So do it!

Japanese: は、はいッ！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥それから。特急代は アンタ持ちやで、ニイちゃん‥‥
English: The special express ain't cheap, Wright! Just so youse know since youse payin'!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ 無法地帯かよ、ここは‥‥）
English: (Oh, man. Doesn't the rule of law mean anything around here!?)

Japanese: ～事件当日‥‥吐麗美庵～
English: -- At Trés Bien --

Japanese: あの日の午後、‥‥レストランで 会うことになっていたんや。
English: I was supposed to meet wid the kid at the restaurant that afternoon.

Japanese: 入り口のドアを開けた瞬間‥‥ イヤなものが見えた。
English: When I opened the door to the joint, I saw one ugly scene.

Japanese: あのワカゾウ、机につっぷして ピクとも動かへんやないか！
English: The guy was laid out over the table, stiff as concrete.

Japanese: こりゃヤバい‥‥ピンと来た。 その場で回れ右、や。
English: I figured if the place wasn't hot already, it was gonna be, so I split.

Japanese: パトカーのサイレンも聞こえたし。 そのまま事務所に帰ったワケや。
English: I heard the cop's sirens on my way out, and I went straight back to my office.

Japanese: ど、どうよ。なるほどくん！
English: Well, Nick? What do you think?

Japanese: そろそろ、ゴマかしきれなく なってきているみたいだ。
English: He's running out of ways to avoid the truth.

Japanese: とにかく、ウソを考える時間を 与えないことだよ。
English: I need to press him fast before he has time to think things through.

Japanese: ムジュンを暴くんだ！ ‥‥振り向きざまに！
English: I've got to come right back at him with a contradiction!

Japanese: ヘタにゆさぶっちゃうと、 ペナルティを食らうしね！
English: Be careful what you press him on, though, or you'll get penalized, OK?

Japanese: 《カリヨーゼ》の事務所じゃ なかったんですか？
English: So the meeting wasn't due to take place at Tender Lender at all then?

Japanese: どうしても、事務所に来たくないと ゴネやがったんや、あのワカゾウ。
English: The kid was makin' a fuss about comin' into de office.

Japanese: なんでやろ。返済のハナシになると みんな、ウチに来たがらへんねや。
English: It's always that way when I wanna talk about repayments.

Japanese: ゴツいサンドバッグもあるのに。
English: Even though I got the best punching bag you' ever seen if there are any "issues".

Japanese: （サンドバッグがあるから じゃないのか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe it's because of the punching bag that people are scared to come?)

Japanese: それで‥‥《吐麗美庵》で 会うコトにしたワケですか。
English: So that's why you decided to meet at Trés Bien?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　まるほどう。 そのハナシ、さっきも聞いたぜ。
English: Ha...! You're going over old ground again, Trite.

Japanese: そ、そうでしたっけ。
English: S-Sorry.

Japanese: ヤクソクどおり、ペナルティを オゴるぜ。‥‥飲みほしな。
English: You just earned yourself a penalty. Now suck it down!

Japanese: 飲みほすように、弁護人。
English: You will suck down the penalty, Mr. Wright. And you will like it!

Japanese: （マイったな‥‥）
English: (Thank you, sir, may I have another!)

Japanese: “イヤなもの”というのは、 いったい‥‥
English: An "ugly scene"? What do you mean?

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずだぜ。イミのない ゆさぶりはユルさねえ、と‥‥。
English: The witness has already told us, Trite. Which makes that question... irrelevant!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥！
English: B-But...!

Japanese: ‥‥オレは、裁判中のコーヒーは １７杯まで、と決めている。
English: I limit myself to 17 cups of coffee during a trial. That's the rule.

Japanese: アンタも、裁判中に食らう ペナルティ、回数を決めることだ。
English: You better limit the number of times you take a penalty, Trite.

Japanese: 胃が、まっ黒になっちゃうぜ！
English: Or your guts will look like the inside of a chimney! Ashen!

Japanese: まっ黒になっちゃいますよ、 弁護人。
English: Don't make me burn you again, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （‥‥今のゆさぶりは 軽はずみだったか‥‥）
English: (I guess I shouldn't have pressed him on that.)

Japanese: どうやら、特急には 乗れそうやなぁ、ニイちゃん‥‥
English: Guess I'm gonna make that special express after all.

Japanese: それで‥‥、あなたが見た “イヤなもの”というのは‥‥？
English: So, to recap... This "ugly scene" you saw was...?

Japanese: ‥‥岡　高夫の死体を 見たわけですか？
English: You mean you saw Glen Elg's dead body?

Japanese: まあ、それらしい背中しか 見えなかったけどな。
English: I guess I did. But I only saw him from behind.

Japanese: たしか、うすぎたない毛糸の キャップをかぶっていたでェ。
English: He was wearin' some raggy bit of cloth he called a hat.

Japanese: それは、何時ごろの ことでしたか？
English: And what time was this?

Japanese: ‥‥知らん。
English: I dunno.
Huh?

Japanese: ワイがこの世で最もユルせんのが、 丸ぅい時計やッ！
English: You' know what winds me up more than anythin' else in the world? Watches! Round watches!

Japanese: そないなモン、 ウデにつけるワケ、あらへん！
English: I ain't gonna pollute my paws wid some tickin' henpecker!

Japanese: あの‥‥ちなみに、２番目に ユルせないのは‥‥？
English: Out of interest, Mr. Tigre, what winds you up the second most?

Japanese: 決まっとるやろがァ！ ‥‥四角ぅい時計やッ。
English: Huh? Whatd'youse think!? Square watches!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Is this guy for real!?)

Japanese: とにかく、何かマズいことが 起こったのはマチガイあらへん！
English: Look, all I needed to know was that somethin' bad was goin' down in that place.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥店の中には 入らなかったワケですね？
English: So you didn't actually set foot inside the restaurant, then?

Japanese: ‥‥ワイの時間は高いからのォ。
English: The Tiger is a busy cat. I don't hang around for no one.

Japanese: ムダな事件に巻きこまれとる ヒマはないんや。
English: I ain't got time to be caught up in no murder investigation.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥このマッチは、 いつもらったんですか？
English: So, when exactly did you pick up the matches?

Japanese: ‥‥マッチは、入り口のそばに 置いてあった。
English: There are matches just inside the front door.

Japanese: ウチの刑事が５個、持って帰ろうと して、シェフに怒られてたぜ。
English: Our detective friend wound up in trouble with the chef after taking 5 books home.

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事‥‥泣かせるなあ）
English: (Poor Gumshoe... It's almost enough to make a man cry...)

Japanese: 何か、やましいコトでも あったんですか！
English: You went straight back? Did a bout of guilt suddenly hit you for what you did?

Japanese: なんやとコラァッ！
English: What're youse trying to say!?

Japanese: やましいコトがないニンゲンが いるかァ、このボケがッ！
English: You' tryin' to tell me youse ain't never been guilty of nothin'?
Um...

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトはだれでも、いくつもの ツミを抱えているんだぜ。
English: We all have our crosses to bear.

Japanese: その暗い闇を、ただ飲みくだして 生きてゆくのさ。
English: We all have to swallow the dark secrets we hide...

Japanese: こんなふうにッ！
English: ...Like this!

Japanese: （‥‥裁判所でする会話とは 思えないな‥‥）
English: (The courtroom's not exactly the place to talk about dark secrets, is it?)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥また やってしまったようですな。
English: It seems you've done it again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: イミのない“ゆさぶり”を。 ‥‥ペナルティを与えます！
English: Another irrelevant line of questioning. I must impose a penalty accordingly!

Japanese: （あの連中の問題発言に ペナルティはないのかよ！）
English: (How about a penalty for those two jokers and the garbage they keep coming up with!?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、 おカネを貸すプロでしたね？
English: You're something of a loan collecting pro, aren't you, Mr. Tigre?

Japanese: せやでェ。このゼニトラから 逃げることはでけへんねん。
English: No one escapes The Tiger's clutches!

Japanese: ゼニトラさん。‥‥ぼくは、 ウソを見破るプロなんですよ。
English: Well I'm something of a lie detecting pro.

Japanese: ぼくから逃げることはできません。
English: And no one escapes The Phoenix's clutches!

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、気づいても いいころですね‥‥。
English: I think it's time we got something straight.

Japanese: どういうコトだ‥‥まるほどう。
English: What's this, Trite? A new line of irrelevant questioning?

Japanese: ここに、現場の上面図があります。
English: These are the floor plans of the crime scene.

Japanese: ‥‥ゼニトラさん。あなたは 入り口に立っていたそうですね。
English: You say you were standing at the entrance, Mr. Tigre?

Japanese: そのポイントから見えるエリアは、 ‥‥こうなります。
English: From there, your field of vision would have covered an area something like... this.

Japanese: たしかに、被害者の席が 目に入りますな‥‥。
English: Indeed. The witness would have had a clear view of the victim's seat.

Japanese: アタリマエや。 ワイは、見たんやからなァ‥‥。
English: Isn't that what I just said? I saw the back of the kid's head.

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥ そうはいきません。
English: Unfortunately for you, that is not possible.

Japanese: ‥‥思い出してください。 店内のテーブルの間には‥‥
English: If the court would think back, you'll remember that between each of the tables...

Japanese: 高い仕切りが立っているんですよ。
English: ...is a tall partition!

Japanese: あ‥‥そ、そういえば‥‥！
English: Why...! That's true!

Japanese: もう一度、上面図を見てください！ ‥‥すべてはアキラカです。
English: Now look at the plans again! The truth is painfully obvious!

Japanese: 入り口のポイントから ジッサイに見えるのは‥‥
English: From the entrance, the field of vision of any customer walking in...

Japanese: ここまでなのです！
English: ...ends here!

Japanese: 吐麗美庵の入り口からでは、 被害者の席は見えない！
English: So, from the entrance of Trés Bien, you couldn't have seen the victim's seat.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは事件当日、 岡　高夫と会っていたのです！
English: But you did see the victim that day... because you met with him!

Japanese: バカな！　きのうのジイさんの 証言を忘れたのか！
English: Wrong! Have you forgotten the old man's testimony yesterday?

Japanese: テーブルには、被害者しか いなかったハズだぜ！
English: The victim was alone at his table.

Japanese: 弁護側は、先ほど 立証したばかりです！
English: But the defense just proved that point to be moot!

Japanese: 五十嵐　将兵の見た“被害者”は ニセモノだった、と！
English: The "victim" witnessed by Mr. Kudo was not Glen Elg, but a fake!

Japanese: なんだと‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: 《真犯人》は、五十嵐さんに まちがった証言をさせるために‥‥
English: In order to have Mr. Kudo falsely testify...

Japanese: 被害者を殺害したあと、彼に なりすまして、シバイをしたッ！
English: ...the real killer posed as the victim he had just killed and acted out a charade!

Japanese: ‥‥そこまで！
English: That will do!

Japanese: 審理がここまで来た以上、 うかがわないわけには行きません。
English: This trial has gone on long enough without the obvious question being answered.

Japanese: その“ニセモノ”になりすました 真犯人の名前を‥‥
English: Who exactly was this "real killer" who impersonated the victim?

Japanese: 《犯人》は、岡　高夫さんを 毒殺したあと‥‥
English: You say the killer murdered Glen Elg...

Japanese: 彼になりすまして、その姿を 五十嵐　将兵に目撃させた。
English: ...and then impersonated his victim in a performance for Victor Kudo?

Japanese: その《真犯人》！ いったい、ダレだったのですか！
English: In that case, Mr. Wright, reveal the identity of this "criminal" to the court!

Japanese: こういう場面では、《意外な人物》 と相場が決まっています！
English: Obviously, it's the person you least expect!

Japanese: あの‥‥。 どうしましたか？　裁判長。
English: Um... What's the matter, Your Honor?

Japanese: あ！　ちょっとボールペンを 落としてしまってですね‥‥
English: Oh, nothing! I just dropped my pen again.

Japanese: 私にかまわず、みなさんで やってください！
English: Don't mind me. Carry on with the proceedings as normal.

Japanese: ボールペンを探しているあいだに もう一度、考えなおそうよ。
English: Better think again while he's down there, Nick.

Japanese: （やっぱり、意外性だけで 選んだのがマズかったか‥‥）
English: (I guess I should've picked the obvious choice...)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、ボールペンも 見つかったので、うかがいます。
English: Very good. I've found my pen now. So, allow me to pose the question again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: もちろん‥‥
English: Obviously...

Japanese: 芝九蔵　虎ノ助さん！ あなたしかいません！
English: ...the killer is Furio Tigre! No one else could have done it.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなのですか！　証人！
English: Wh-What!? Well, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥カカカカカカカカ‥‥‥‥ オモロイやないかい。
English: Grrrah ha hah! Now that's cute!

Japanese: このゼニトラに 言いがかりをつけるたぁ‥‥
English: Youse think you can pin this on The Tiger!?

Japanese: ‥‥命知らずやのお‥‥‥‥
English: Maybe youse don't understand. The Tiger is king of the jungle!

Japanese: ダレやァッ！　いったいダレが、 ワイとやり合うっちゅうんじゃい！
English: So I dares you' to say it again! Come on! You' got the guts!?

Japanese: わわ、ワレワレをオドしても む、む、む、ムダですぞ！
English: Y-Y-You can't threaten m-me, Mr. Tigre!

Japanese: ホレ、弁護人！ カツンと言っておやりなさいッ！
English: I-It's the defense! Go ahead and tell the witness, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くんんんんんッ！
English: Mr. Wriiight!

Japanese: そうか‥‥アンタか、ジイさん。 ‥‥エエ度胸やあああァァァッ！
English: Sounds to me like it must be you', old man. Youse got guts, I'll give you that.

Japanese: べべべべ、弁護人！ 私をヒトリにしないように！
English: M-M-Mr. Wright! Do not leave me to handle this alone!

Japanese: 情けねェ連中だぜ‥‥
English: Perhaps I can end this embarrassment.

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事ぃぃ！
English: M-Mr. Godot!

Japanese: ‥‥よおく思い出すんだな。 五十嵐　将兵の証言を、もう一度。
English: Let's just go back over Mr. Kudo's testimony one more time.

Japanese: ジイさんが目撃したのは、 被害者だけじゃねえんだぜ！
English: The old man didn't see just the victim! Oh, no, no, no.

Japanese: 女給さんがコーヒーを運んできた のだが‥‥中に、何か入れたのだ！
English: The serving girl brought him a javaccino... But she put something in it!

Japanese: まちがいない！　ワシに見せつける かのように、白いコナをッ！
English: There's no question about it! She very conspicuously put some white powder in there!

Japanese: 被害者がニセモノかどうか‥‥？ そんなコトは関係ねえのさ。
English: Was the victim he saw the real victim or not? That doesn't matter.

Japanese: カップに毒を入れたのが、 被告人である以上は！
English: The fact remains, he saw the accused put the poison into the coffee!

Japanese: い、言われてみれば‥‥たしかに、 毒を入れたのはウエイトレスの
English: Yes, it was the waitress who poisoned the coffee--

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくのルスを狙うとは、 やりますね。ゴドー検事。
English: V-Very impressive, Mr. Godot. ...Waiting for my absence to launch your attack!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
ボールペンは見つかったかい、 まるほどう‥‥
English: Found your pen at last, Trite?

Japanese: ポケットにさしてありました。
English: It was in my pocket.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。
English: *ahem* But anyway...

Japanese: イガラシさんが目撃した 人物は‥‥２人。
English: Mr. Kudo witnessed two people that day.

Japanese: 被害者の“岡　高夫”と、 後ろ姿の“ウエイトレス”‥‥。
English: He saw the victim, the "supposed" Mr. Glen Elg, and the "waitress"... from behind.

Japanese: そのとおりですが‥‥
English: Yes... Your point, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥考えるまでも ないじゃないですか。
English: I think the conclusion is obvious.

Japanese: “岡　高夫”が、犯人による シバイだったのなら‥‥
English: If this "Glen Elg" was really the killer in disguise...

Japanese: 当然‥‥このウエイトレスも、 シバイだったのではないか‥‥？
English: ...then surely it's possible the "waitress" was also part of the show.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！ ウエイトレスも‥‥ニセモノ！
English: What!? You mean, the waitress was an imposter as well!?

Japanese: 須々木　マコは、事件発生の直後、 “キゼツした”と証言している！
English: The defendant, Ms. Byrde, fell unconscious immediately after the incident.

Japanese: すべては‥‥被告人の意識のそとで 仕組まれた、ワナだったのです！
English: And someone used her fainting to hatch an elaborate plan to pin the murder on her!

Japanese: いったい‥‥ダレだったのですか！
English: Who on earth was it!?

Japanese: 五十嵐　将兵が目撃した “ウエイトレス”の正体は‥‥！
English: Who was this "waitress" that Mr. Kudo witnessed?

Japanese: こういう場面では、《意外な人物》 と相場が決まっています！
English: Obviously, it's the person you least expect!

Japanese: あの‥‥。 どうしましたか？　裁判長。
English: Um... What's the matter, Your Honor?

Japanese: あ！　ちょっとボールペンを 落としてしまってですね‥‥
English: Oh, nothing! I just dropped my pen again.

Japanese: 私にかまわず、みなさんで やってください！
English: Don't mind me. Carry on with the proceedings as normal.

Japanese: ボールペンを探しているあいだに もう一度、考えなおそうよ。
English: Better think again while he's down there, Nick.

Japanese: （やっぱり、意外性だけで 選んだのがマズかったか‥‥）
English: (I guess I should've picked the obvious choice...)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、ボールペンも 見つかったので、うかがいます。
English: Very good. I've found my pen now. So, allow me to pose the question again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ど‥‥ どなたですか？　この方は‥‥
English: W-Who is this woman?

Japanese: ‥‥鹿羽　うらみ。 《カリヨーゼ》の従業員、です。
English: Her name is Viola Cadaverini. She's an employee of Tender Lender.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ‥‥
English: Youse makin' a big mistake...

Japanese: うらみちゃんが、ダレのマゴか、 知っとるやろうなァ‥‥
English: D'you know who Violetta's grandfather is?

Japanese: 今日、裁判所からブジに 帰れると思わんほうがええでェ‥‥
English: You' betta be goin' home in an armored truck tonight, if you' know what I mean...

Japanese: ふるえちゃダメ！　なるほどくん！
English: Stop shaking, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥と、とにかく！
English: Wh-Where was I?

Japanese: 被告人・須々木　マコは、 こんな証言をしています！
English: Yes, the defendant, Ms. Byrde, has stated the following...

Japanese: スズキが、あのテーブルに コーヒーを運んだとき‥‥
English: Well, when I took the coffee over to the victim's table...

Japanese: 店内には、お客さんが もう１人、いたッス。
English: ...it's true there was another customer in the restaurant.

Japanese: たしか、ちょっと ジメッとしていて‥‥
English: Umm... She was sort of creepy...

Japanese: イミありげに“ククッ‥‥”と 笑っていたッス。
English: And she had a kind of cackling laugh.

Japanese: ‥‥この事件には、 ムジュンが多すぎました。
English: There are just too many contradictions in this case.

Japanese: 被害者のテーブルから 消えた“もう１人のオトコ”‥‥
English: The "second man" at the victim's table who nobody but Ms. Byrde seems to have seen.

Japanese: 聞こえるはずのない耳に ついていたヘッドホン‥‥
English: The earpiece worn by the victim in his left ear when that eardrum was ruptured!

Japanese: とっくに終わっていたはずの ラジオ番組‥‥
English: And the radio show he was supposedly listening to half an hour after it was over.

Japanese: すべてにスジが通る コタエは、たった１つです。
English: There is only one logical explanation that clears up all of these contradictions!

Japanese: 事件は、２回起こった！ ‥‥ホンモノと、ニセモノと‥‥。
English: The whole incident took place twice! Once for real, and once for show!

Japanese: そして‥‥犯行が可能だったのは、 ‥‥芝九蔵　虎ノ助さん。
English: And, Mr. Furio Tigre, the only person who could have committed the crime...

Japanese: あなたしかいない！
English: ...was you!

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人。 いかがですかな‥‥？
English: W-Witness? What have you got to say?
That's cute!
S-Sorry?

Japanese: アンタら、なかなかの 役者やのォ‥‥
English: Youse alright. I could do with a guy like you' around.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトですか？
English: Wh-What do you mean?

Japanese: ええワ！　つきあったる！
English: OK, I'm in on this game!

Japanese: 取引先まで、ジェット機を チャーターせにゃならんが‥‥
English: I'm gonna have to charter a jet to get me to my meetin' now, but...

Japanese: 最後にもう一度だけ、 オモロイこと、聞かしたるわ！
English: ...I'm gonna give youse one more thing to think about before I go.

Japanese: お、“オモロイこと”‥‥？
English: Something to think about?

Japanese: ニイちゃん、もっともらしいコト 言うとるがなァ‥‥
English: Youse got it all wrapped up nice, huh, Wright?

Japanese: とんでもない見落としを しとるっちゅうコトやッ！
English: But youse missed out one real important thing!

Japanese: そ、そんなハズは‥‥！
English: But that can't be!

Japanese: ‥‥ワイはたしかに、あの日。 あのワカゾウと会ったワ。
English: I was in the joint that day. And I met that kid, too.

Japanese: せやけど‥‥ワイが、ワカゾウに 毒を盛るハズがないんや。
English: But I couldn't have poisoned him, youse hear?

Japanese: な、なんですと‥‥！ それは、いったいなぜ‥‥
English: What!? Do you really expect us to believe you now, Mr. Tigre?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
‥‥まったく、とんだ いたずらボウヤだぜ。
English: What a troublemaker.

Japanese: い、いたずら‥‥
English: T-Troublemaker...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥最後に もう一度、必要みたいだぜ。
English: Looks like we're going to need another one for the road.

Japanese: とびきりホットな《証言》が‥‥！
English: One more steaming cup of hot testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥そのようですな。 それでは、証人。
English: Indeed. Witness, you will explain yourself to the court.

Japanese: もう一度だけ、 証言していただきましょう。
English: I will give you one more chance to testify.

Japanese: 事件当日‥‥吐麗美庵で 被害者と、何があったか‥‥？
English: What happened that day at Trés Bien between yourself and the victim?

Japanese: ～被害者との関係について～
English: -- Ties to the Victim --

Japanese: 岡　高夫には、カネを貸してたんや ‥‥１０００万円ほどなァ。
English: Yeah, I loaned Elg cash. About 100,000 dollars.

Japanese: あの日は、融資の話やなかった。 返済の期限日やったんや。
English: That day, we was due to have a little chat. The kid had hit his payback date, see?

Japanese: ところが、あのワカゾウ。 カネがないと言いだしやがった。
English: So anyway, he tells me he's got no way to pay up.

Japanese: ペチャンコにしたろ思ったとき。 突然、アイツが騒ぎ出したんや。
English: I'm about to flatten the guy, when he starts screamin'.

Japanese: “５０００万円当たったァ” ‥‥ってな。運のいいやっちゃア。
English: "Yesss! I won! Half a million bucks!" He got lucky, you' know. Real lucky.

Japanese: ‥‥あのウエイトレスが妙なコトを しなければ、丸く収まってたんや。
English: If that waitress hadn't done what she done, everythin' woulda been over.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。借金の額面は ５００万円、ですか。
English: Now, I see that the principal amount you loaned to Mr. Elg was 50,000 dollars...

Japanese: まあ‥‥利子やらナニやらで 少し、ふくらんだワケやな。
English: Yeah, well. Youse got the vig to take into account. Interest builds up fast, you' know.

Japanese: （１０００万円って、２バイ じゃないか‥‥）
English: (That's faster than fast! 100,000 dollars is twice his principal!)

Japanese: 返済期限は、１２月３日。 たしかに、事件当日です。
English: And the repayment deadline was December 3rd, the day of the incident in question.

Japanese: ‥‥まったく、 とんだシンデレラ・ボーイやで。
English: Yeah. He was one lucky kid!

Japanese: あのドタンバで、５０００万円 当てるとはなァ。
English: He got that half a million just in time.

Japanese: そう‥‥。ワイには、 《理由》がないんや。
English: So I ain't have no reason to kill the kid.

Japanese: ‥‥あのワカゾウを殺す、 《動機》ってヤツがなァ‥‥！
English: And if I ain't got no motive, you ain't got no case!

Japanese: （芝九蔵　虎ノ助の “動機”‥‥か！）
English: (His motive? Hmm... He has to have one, but what is it?)

Japanese: ～被害者との関係について～
English: -- Ties to the Victim --

Japanese: 岡　高夫には、カネを貸してたんや ‥‥１０００万円ほどなァ。
English: Yeah, I loaned Elg cash. About 100,000 dollars.

Japanese: あの日は、融資の話やなかった。 返済の期限日やったんや。
English: That day, we was due to have a little chat. The kid had hit his payback date, see?

Japanese: ところが、あのワカゾウ。 カネがないと言いだしやがった。
English: So anyway, he tells me he's got no way to pay up.

Japanese: ペチャンコにしたろ思ったとき。 突然、アイツが騒ぎ出したんや。
English: I'm about to flatten the guy, when he starts screamin'.

Japanese: “５０００万円当たったァ” ‥‥ってな。運のいいやっちャア。
English: "Yesss! I won! Half a million bucks!" He got lucky, you' know. Real lucky.

Japanese: ‥‥あのウエイトレスが妙なコトを しなければ、丸く収まってたんや。
English: If that waitress hadn't done what she done, everythin' woulda been over.

Japanese: ゼニトラ、おカネを返して もらうつもりだったんだよね？
English: So The Tiger was meeting Glen Elg to get his money back.

Japanese: それなら、たしかに‥‥ クジをもらえば済むハナシだよ！
English: If Mr. Elg had just given him the lottery ticket, that would have been the end of it.

Japanese: でも、それじゃあ 殺人の動機にはならないよ。
English: Yeah, so what's The Tiger's motive?

Japanese: （‥‥ここが、 最大の勝負どころだ！）
English: (This is the big one.)

Japanese: （この証言をくずさなければ ‥‥勝てない‥‥！）
English: (If I don't manage to undermine this testimony, it's all over for us.)

Japanese: なぜ、岡　高夫に毒を 盛らなければならなかったのか？
English: Why would he have to poison Mr. Elg?

Japanese: そして、そもそも‥‥なぜ、返せる アテもない男に、カネを貸したか？
English: And why did he loan him the money in the first place, when he knew he couldn't repay him?

Japanese: 被告人が毒を入れてくれたおかげで ワイの取り引きはパアや！
English: Thanks to what she did, my business with that kid was over!

Japanese: ワイの目当ては、１０００万円や。 他にヤツを殺す理由、あらへんで！
English: I was after the $100,000. I didn't have no other reason to kill the guy!

Japanese: ‥‥返せるアテはあったんですか？
English: Did he have any way of paying the loan back?

Japanese: まあ‥‥あのワカゾウは なかなかのギャンブラーやった。
English: The fool was a gambler.

Japanese: “デカいのをイッパツ当てるまで、 やめられない”なんて言うて。
English: He said he couldn't give it up 'til he landed a big win.

Japanese: ‥‥だからワイも、できるかぎり 協力してやったんや。
English: So I agreed to help him.

Japanese: ‥‥協力？　あなたが？
English: Help him? You?

Japanese: デカいギャンブルのハナシを 次々に持ちかけてやったワケや。
English: I kept hittin' him with ideas for ways he could getta big win.

Japanese: ‥‥ゼンブ負けたみたいやけど。
English: But the guy kept losin'.

Japanese: それは‥‥やっぱり、 岡　高夫さんのためを思って‥‥？
English: So you were helping him for his sake? Or yours?

Japanese: まあ‥‥。ウチのモットーは 《三日坊主》やからなァ。
English: "Win Through Compromise"! Youse help me, I help youse! What's the difference, huh?
(...I don't believe this!)

Japanese: でも‥‥返せるアテのないヒトに おカネなんか貸すかなあ。
English: Nick, would anyone really ever loan money to someone they thought was unreliable?

Japanese: あたし、なるほどくんには ５００円までしか貸さないから。
English: Like, for example, if it were you, I'd only loan you five bucks, max.

Japanese: （‥‥シツレイな子だな）
English: (Thanks a lot, Maya.)

Japanese: 期限日‥‥というと、いくらぐらい 返すヤクソクだったんですか？
English: So how much were you expecting him to pay you back on that day?

Japanese: 利息をふくめた１０００万円、 全額に決まっとるやろ！
English: What d'youse think!? The whole package! $100,000, including interest.

Japanese: ヘビー級のパンチだね、それは‥‥
English: That's a real heavyweight punch!

Japanese: 月々の返済が、１回でも おくれたら、全額いただく！
English: Once a client misses a repayment, you' call the whole loan in.

Japanese: ‥‥金融界のジョーシキやで、 ホンマ、コイツだけは。
English: You' wanna make it in my world, that's all youse gotta know.

Japanese: あの。何回目の返済で おくれたんですか？　被害者は。
English: And how many times had Mr. Elg been late with his repayments?

Japanese: １回目や。
English: Once.

Japanese: ‥‥月々、いくらずつ 返すハズだったんですか？
English: And how much was he supposed to pay back every month?
50 bucks.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、岡　高夫さんが ワルモノに見えてきたよ。
English: Sounds like Mr. Elg was in a real sticky spot.

Japanese: （まあ、５０００円ずつ返しても 借金、へらないんだけどな）
English: (Yeah, at 50 dollars a month, he'd never pay that huge loan off!)

Japanese: あの‥‥ いくら足りなかったんですか？
English: How much did he have left on his debt?

Japanese: おおまかに言って‥‥ １０００万円やな。
English: You want it in round figures? About 100,000 bucks.

Japanese: （全額じゃないか！）
English: (That's the whole amount!)

Japanese: ‥‥死亡当時、被害者のサイフには ５８円しか入っていなかったぜ。
English: We're talking about a guy who had 58 cents in his wallet.

Japanese: なんや！　アイツ、コーヒー代 払うつもり、なかったんかい！
English: What!? Youse tellin' me he wasn't even gonna pay for the coffee!?

Japanese: ものスゴい度胸ですな‥‥。
English: He certainly seems to have been a brave man, this Mr. Elg.

Japanese: ミョーに落ちついとったんや、 あのオトコ‥‥。
English: That guy was smooth, I tell youse. Real smooth!

Japanese: “あと５分以内に、カネは返す” ‥‥なんて言うて。
English: "You'll have your money in less than 5 minutes!" That's what he says to me.

Japanese: “ホトケのゼニトラ”と言われた ワイも、それはゴキゲンナナメや。
English: The guy then calls me the "Tender Tiger". He was skatin' on thin ice with me!

Japanese: もしかして、それが‥‥？
English: Was that because he...?

Japanese: ‥‥宝クジや。 ヤツの最後の大勝負やった。
English: The guy won the lottery. Was his last chance at a big win.

Japanese: そのクジは‥‥ もちろん、これですね？
English: And you can confirm that this is the ticket in question?

Japanese: ‥‥せや。
English: ...That's it.

Japanese: 《長者ラジオ》の番組は、 午後１時半から１０分間‥‥。
English: The "Millionaire Radio" show starts at 1:30 PM and runs for 10 minutes...

Japanese: どうやら、２人が会っていた 時間が、ハッキリしましたな。
English: That fixes the time you two met with some accuracy.

Japanese: （このときのシーンが、３０分後に くり返されたワケだな）
English: (And the whole scene was acted out again 30 minutes later.)

Japanese: （‥‥五十嵐　将兵に 見せるために‥‥！）
English: (All so that Mr. Kudo would see it!)

Japanese: あのときのワカゾウのカオ‥‥ 今でも忘れんのォ。
English: I can still see the kid's face now. I ain't never gonna forget it.

Japanese: そのとき、店には他に だれかいましたか？
English: Was there anyone else in the restaurant at the time?

Japanese: ‥‥さあなあ‥‥ おぼえてへんワ。
English: I don't remember.

Japanese: （ジッサイには、 このとき、店内には‥‥）
English: (If there was no one there, I'll wear that ridiculous tiger shirt for a month!)

Japanese: （本土坊さんに、マコちゃん‥‥ そして、うらみさんがいたはず）
English: (Mr. Armstrong, Maggey and if I'm right, Viola Cadaverini, were all there at that time.)

Japanese: では‥‥被害者は、最初から 宝クジで借金を返そうと‥‥？
English: So, the victim had intended to repay you from his lottery winnings from the beginning?

Japanese: そのツモリやったみたいやで。
English: Seems dat way to me.

Japanese: 当たるワケないでしょう、 フツウは‥‥！
English: B-But you wouldn't normally expect to win the lottery, would you!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
絶対的なピンチにいても、 自分の勝ちしかイメージしない。
English: The undying belief that your next roll will end the worst losing streak you ever had...

Japanese: それがギャンブラー‥‥だぜ。
English: That's what defines a true gambler.

Japanese: （カッコイイな、なんか）
English: (He makes it sound so cool...)

Japanese: ダメだよ、ナットクしちゃ。 なるほどくん、弱いんだから！
English: Don't be tempted, Nick! You haven't got the will- power for it!

Japanese: とにかく‥‥最後の大勝負で、 アイツはついに、勝ったんや。
English: All I know is, the kid took a shot and he got lucky in the end.

Japanese: ウエイトレス、というのは‥‥？
English: The waitress. You mean...?

Japanese: 被告席でちっちゃくなってる メガネに決まっとるやろがァ！
English: The girl with the glasses in the defendant's chair! Who else could I mean!?

Japanese: ソイツのせいで、丸く収まるモンが メチャメチャになったんや！
English: If she hadn't got in the way, things woulda been, bada-bing, bada-boom, over and done with!

Japanese: （もっと‥‥詳しくハナシを 聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe I should push a little on this...)

Japanese: 被告人が‥‥ナニをしたと 言うんですか？
English: What exactly are you implying the defendant did?

Japanese: あのメガネ、聞いとったんや！ ５０００万円のクジのコトをなァ！
English: How about you go ask four-eyes about that half a million dollar ticket!

Japanese: それが、どうしても ほしくなった。だから‥‥
English: She wanted it so bad...

Japanese: コーヒーに毒を入れたワケや！
English: ...she poisoned Elg's coffee.

Japanese: そ‥‥それは、証人の 勝手な言いぶんです！
English: A likely theory! Your word hasn't held water lately, Mr. Tigre.

Japanese: ‥‥そうは行かねえさ。 この証人は、現場にいたんだ。
English: Let's not forget this witness was actually at the scene, Trite.

Japanese: ワイは、闇金融なんて商売を しとるからのお。
English: The law don't exactly agree with some of the deals I send down.

Japanese: 現場にいるワケにはいかん。 急いで立ち去るしかなかったんや。
English: I couldn't be there when de cops showed up, so I split.

Japanese: そ、そうだったのですか‥‥。
English: I-I see...

Japanese: 裁判長！　今の目撃証言‥‥ コーヒー２杯ぶんの価値があるぜ。
English: Your Honor! The witness's last few statements are worth a good two cups of coffee.

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！ 証人、証言を修正してください！
English: I concur, Mr. Godot! You will amend your testimony accordingly, witness!

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥そういうコトや。 成歩堂　龍一ィィ‥‥
English: Ha ha ha! So that's what youse after, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: “丸く収まった”というのは？
English: What do you mean things would have been over and done with?

Japanese: アホか、アンタ。 借金に決まっとるやろうが！
English: Are youse all there or what!? I'm talkin' about the cash!

Japanese: ワイは‥‥その。１０００万円さえ 返してもらえれば、よかったんや。
English: I was there to get my 100,000 bucks back. That's all. I'm a business man.

Japanese: それを、あのウエイトレスが ジャマしよってからにィィ‥‥
English: It was all comin' together before that waitress got in the way.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥。 証言を聞いているかぎり、
English: Hmm... As far as I can tell from the witness's testimony...

Japanese: 証人にとっての《動機》は、 その借金以外、なさそうです。
English: ...other than recouping his loan, Mr. Tigre had no motive for killing the victim.

Japanese: 証人！　今の発言をふまえて、 証言を修正してください！
English: Witness! You will amend your testimony to reflect what you just said.

Japanese: （‥‥ゼニトラの“動機”か‥‥）
English: (The Tiger's motive, huh...)

Japanese: （ゼニトラは、マコちゃんに ツミを着せようとしている）
English: (The Tiger's trying to pin the crime on Maggey.)

Japanese: （目撃したことについて聞くのは、 こちらに不利なだけだな‥‥）
English: (If I ask him about what he saw, it's only going to damage our case.)

Japanese: その‥‥“パアになった取り引き” というのは？
English: What do you mean things would have been over and done with?

Japanese: アホか、アンタ。 借金に決まっとるやろうが！
English: Are youse all there or what!? I'm talkin' about the cash!

Japanese: ワイは‥‥その。１０００万円さえ 返してもらえれば、よかったんや。
English: I was there to get my 100,000 bucks back. That's all. I'm a business man.

Japanese: それを、あのウエイトレスが ジャマしよってからにィィ‥‥
English: It was all comin' together before that waitress got in the way.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥。 証言を聞いているかぎり、
English: Hmm... As far as I can tell from the witness's testimony...

Japanese: 証人にとっての《動機》は、 その借金以外、なさそうです。
English: ...other than recouping his loan, Mr. Tigre had no motive for killing the victim.

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。 証言を修正してもらいますかな？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Are you happy with the testimony, or would you like it amended?

Japanese: （このままの証言で、 もう少し考えてみよう‥‥）
English: (I better leave it at that and try to figure something out...)

Japanese: ‥‥けっこうです、裁判長。
English: Thank you, Your Honor, but there is no need to amend the testimony.

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥では、尋問をつづけましょう。
English: Very well. Continue with your cross- examination, then.

Japanese: （このままの証言じゃ、 ラチがあかないな‥‥）
English: (I'm not going to get anywhere with the testimony as it stands...)

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいします、裁判長。
English: Your Honor, the defense would like the testimony amended.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 証人！　今の発言をふまえて、 証言を修正してください！
English: Witness! You will amend your testimony to reflect your recent statements, please.

Japanese: “他に、動機はない” ‥‥そういうことですか？
English: Are you saying you had no other motives that day?

Japanese: モチロンや。
English: Yeah, so what?

Japanese: それなら‥‥その当たりクジ、 なぜ持って帰らなかったんだ？
English: Then why didn't you take the lottery ticket when you left?

Japanese: あの、メガネのウエイトレスの せいに、決まっとるやろがァ！
English: Why d'youse think!? Because of four-eyes over there!

Japanese: （マコちゃん、か‥‥）
English: (Because of Maggey!?)

Japanese: あのメガネ、聞いとったんや！ ５０００万円のクジのコトをなァ！
English: How about you go ask four-eyes about that half a million dollar ticket!

Japanese: それが、どうしても ほしくなった。だから‥‥
English: She wanted it so bad...

Japanese: コーヒーに毒を入れたワケや！
English: ...she poisoned Elg's coffee.

Japanese: そ‥‥それは、証人の 勝手な言いぶんです！
English: A likely theory! Your word hasn't held water lately, Mr. Tigre.

Japanese: ‥‥そうは行かねえさ。 この証人は、現場にいたんだ。
English: Let's not forget this witness was actually at the scene, Trite.

Japanese: ワイは、闇金融なんて商売を しとるからのお。
English: The law don't exactly agree with some of the deals I send down.

Japanese: 現場にいるワケにはいかん。 急いで立ち去るしかなかったんや。
English: I couldn't be there when de cops showed up, so I split.

Japanese: そ、そうだったのですか‥‥。
English: I-I see...

Japanese: 裁判長！　今の目撃証言‥‥ コーヒー２杯ぶんの価値があるぜ。
English: Your Honor! The witness's last few statements are worth a good two cups of coffee.

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。 証言を修正してもらいますかな？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Are you happy with the testimony, or would you like it amended?

Japanese: （このままの証言じゃ、 ラチがあかないな‥‥）
English: (I'm not going to get anywhere with the testimony as it stands...)

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいします、裁判長。
English: Your Honor, the defense would like the testimony amended.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 証人！　今の発言をふまえて、 証言を修正してください！
English: Witness! You will amend your testimony to reflect your recent statements, please.

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんから、１０００万円を 返してもらうつもりだった、と？
English: So you just intended to get back the 100,000 dollars Mr. Elg owed you, correct?

Japanese: そうや。ワイには、 そのケンリがあるからのォ。
English: I loaned the guy the cash, so that's my right.

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら‥‥
English: Unfortunately for Mr. Elg...

Japanese: １０００万円では、足りなかった。 ‥‥それはもう、ゼンゼン。
English: ...100,000 dollars wasn't enough to cover it, was it?

Japanese: なんやて‥‥
English: Huh?

Japanese: 今から半年ほど前‥‥ あなたは、交通事故を起こした。
English: About 6 months ago, you were involved in a traffic accident, weren't you?

Japanese: スクーターとクルマの接触事故。 ひとりの女性が、ケガを負った‥‥
English: An accident involving a car and a scooter, in which a young woman was injured.

Japanese: 女性は病院に運びこまれて、 手術を受けたそうです。
English: She was taken to the hospital, where she underwent surgery.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、アンタ‥‥ どこまで知っとるんやァ‥‥
English: Wh-Where did youse get all this info...?

Japanese: これが、そのときケガを負わせた 女性の診断書です。
English: These medical papers document the treatment of the young woman in question.

Japanese: 手術代として‥‥１億円が 請求されています。
English: According to these, her operation cost... one million dollars!

Japanese: 支払い期限は、去年の１２月中‥‥ どうやら、払えたようですね。
English: Payment for these expenses was due in December of last year, and was paid in full!

Japanese: い‥‥イチオクエン‥‥！ な、なんとバカげた‥‥
English: O-One million dollars!? A preposterous sum! Someone should sue these HMOs!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　そんな請求、 マジメに払うヤツはいねえぜ。
English: Ha...! No one would pay a bill like that.

Japanese: 医師会に持ちこめば、 その病院はアウト‥‥だ。
English: If the medical association got wind of it, the hospital would end up as dead as a morgue.

Japanese: その金額がいくらであろうと、 彼は支払うしかなかった！
English: But Mr. Tigre had no choice but to pay!

Japanese: なにしろ、自分の命が かかっているのですからね！
English: Because his very life depended on it!

Japanese: ぐわおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Grrraaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ い‥‥“命”というのは‥‥！
English: O-Order! Order! Order! You say his life depended on it, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: 《ケガをさせた女性》‥‥それが、 鹿羽　うらみさんだったのです。
English: Indeed it did. Simply because the injured woman was none other than Viola Cadaverini.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ “シカバネ”‥‥だと？
English: Did you say, "Cadaverini"?

Japanese: この街の闇金融のボス、 《鹿羽組》組長・鹿羽　権太。
English: Bruto Cadaverini. Mob boss in charge of all underworld activities in the city, and...

Japanese: 鹿羽　うらみさんは、 そのマゴ娘です。
English: ...doting grandfather to his precious "Violetta", also known as Viola Cadaverini.

Japanese: ぐふおおおおおおッ！
English: Bwooooar!

Japanese: “オトシマエ”をつけなければ、 命があぶない。‥‥だからこそ！
English: Your life was in danger unless you paid "compensation" to the boss, correct? It makes sense!

Japanese: あなたは、なんとしても １億円を用意するしかなかった！
English: You were desperate to acquire the one million dollars Cadaverini demanded of you!

Japanese: そのために、岡　高夫さんの 命をギセイにしたのです！
English: So desperate, in fact, that you decided to sacrifice Glen Elg's life to pay your debt!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ しょ‥‥証人！　どうなのですか！
English: Order! Order! Order! What do you say to that, witness?
...Grrr... urgh...

Japanese: ‥‥オトナのおとぎ話は そこまでだぜ、まるほどう‥‥
English: It's a nice little fairy tale, Trite.

Japanese: ‥‥仮に、この証人が１億円を 必要としていたとしても‥‥
English: But even if the witness did need a million dollars...

Japanese: そいつは、岡　高夫の殺害とは 結びつかねえ！
English: ...that doesn't tie him to Mr. Elg's murder.

Japanese: ヤツに貸した金は１０００万円！ しかも、返せるアテはなかった！
English: Mr. Elg only owed him 100,000 dollars, and had no hope of repaying it anyway!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうや！　あんなオトコ、 クソほどの値打ちもあらへん！
English: Yeah. That guy wasn't worth my spit!

Japanese: ‥‥そうじゃない。 “逆”なんですよ。
English: I don't think so. In fact, the opposite is true.

Japanese: ぎゃ‥‥“逆”‥‥だと？
English: The opposite?

Japanese: 返せるアテのない人間にカネを 貸すなど、本来はあり得ない。
English: Lending money with no hope of ever seeing repayment would normally be bad for business.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: この場合は、“返すアテがない” ことこそが、重要でした。
English: In this case, the very fact that Glen Elg had no way to repay the money is crucial.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: 自分がナニを言ってるか、 わかってるの！　なるほどくん！
English: Do you know where you're going with this, Nick?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。‥‥発想を “逆転”させるんだ。
English: You've got to think outside the box, Maya.

Japanese: ぎゃくてん‥‥
English: Outside the box?

Japanese: カンタンなコトさ。芝九蔵の狙いは １０００万円じゃなかったんだ。
English: It's very straightforward. Mr. Tigre wasn't after the 100,000 dollars at all.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人には、何か 考えがあるようですね。
English: It seems you have a logical conclusion for this theory, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ここで、提示して いただきましょう。
English: Would you care to share it with us?

Japanese: いったい‥‥この証人の“狙い” とは、なんだったのか！
English: What exactly was Mr. Tigre "after"?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか、 この証拠品は。
English: What is the meaning of this, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: 弁護人が、裁判所に行ったことが ないことを示す証拠品だろうぜ。
English: It's evidence... that Trite has no idea what he's doing.

Japanese: な、何を言ってるんですか！ ぼくは、ここにこうして‥‥
English: What!? What do you mean?

Japanese: それだけ、マトはずれな 証拠だと言ってるんだ！
English: You're way off track, Trite. That evidence is irrelevant!

Japanese: （なんでコイツに ペナルティを食らうんだよ‥‥）
English: (Since when did he have the right to dish out penalties?)

Japanese: だ、だいじょうぶなの、 なるほどくん！
English: A-Are you alright, Nick?

Japanese: 芝九蔵が必要だったのは、 あくまでも１億円だ。
English: Mr. Tigre needed one million dollars.

Japanese: １０００万円を返してもらうだけ じゃ、ダメだったんだ！
English: Recovering a loan worth 100,000 dollars wasn't enough.

Japanese: （岡　高夫には、その大金を 生み出すウデがあった‥‥）
English: (Besides, Elg had the means to make that kind of money if he wanted to.)

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんは、 優秀なプログラマーでした。
English: Glen Elg was a programmer. A highly skilled programmer.

Japanese: この証人は、そのウデを 担保に、カネを貸したのです。
English: That skill was the collateral Mr. Elg put up in order to borrow the money.

Japanese: プログラマーの‥‥“ウデ”‥‥
English: Care to explain how skill can be used as collateral to secure a loan, Trite?

Japanese: コンピュータ・ウイルスです。 《クリーニング・ボンバー》！
English: He had the skill to create a computer virus, and so he did. A virus known as "MC Bomber"!

Japanese: こんぴゅーた・ういるす‥‥ いったい、それはッ！
English: A "computer virus"? What does one of those do?

Japanese: コンピュータの内部を 破壊するプログラムです。
English: A computer virus is a program that wreaks havoc on the insides of a computer.

Japanese: その“こんぴゅーた”というのは、 いったい‥‥？
English: A "computer"? What does one of those do?

Japanese: （そこから わからないのかよ！）
English: (I guess the beard isn't the only part of His Honor that is from the stone age!)

Japanese: あとで、ゆっくり説明します。 とにかく、重要なポイントは‥‥
English: I'll explain it to you later, Your Honor. Right now, this is the important point...

Japanese: このプログラムに、数億円の 価値がある、ということです！
English: A virus like "MC Bomber" would be worth several million dollars on the black market.

Japanese: 芝九蔵さん！
English: Mr. Tigre!

Japanese: あなたは、１０００万円など どうでもよかった！
English: You couldn't have cared less about the 100,000 dollars! Isn't that right?

Japanese: 狙いは、ただ１つ！　この プログラムだったのです！
English: All you cared about was one thing! The virus!
Grrr...

Japanese: ぐわああああああああああああッ！
English: Grrraaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんから、１０００万円を 返してもらうつもりだった、と？
English: So you just intended to get back the 100,000 dollars Mr. Elg owed you, correct?

Japanese: そうや。ワイには、その ケンリがあるからのォ。
English: I loaned the guy the cash, so that's my right.

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら‥‥
English: Unfortunately for Mr. Elg...

Japanese: あなたの狙いは、１０００万円 なんかではなかったハズです。
English: I don't believe the 100,000 dollars is what you were after.

Japanese: どういうことだ‥‥まるほどう。
English: What are you getting at, Trite?

Japanese: 金貸しに、カネ以外 なんの狙いがあるというんだ！
English: What else would a moneylender be after other than money!?

Japanese: カネはカネでも‥‥ ケタがちがったんですよ。
English: Oh, the moneylender was after money. But money in a totally different league.

Japanese: この、たった１枚のディスクに、 その価値があるのです！
English: The kind of money that a single disc like this would fetch!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: What is that?

Japanese: コンピュータ・ウイルスです。 《クリーニング・ボンバー》！
English: A computer virus, Your Honor. A virus called "MC Bomber"!

Japanese: こんぴゅーた・ういるす‥‥ いったい、それはッ！
English: A "computer virus"? What does one of those do?

Japanese: コンピュータの内部を 破壊するプログラムです。
English: A computer virus is a program that wreaks havoc on the insides of a computer.

Japanese: その“こんぴゅーた”というのは、 いったい‥‥？
English: A "computer"? What does one of those do?

Japanese: （そこから わからないのかよ！）
English: (I guess the beard isn't the only part of His Honor that is from the stone age!)

Japanese: あとで、ゆっくり説明します。 とにかく、重要なポイントは‥‥
English: I'll explain it to you later, Your Honor. Right now, this is the important point...

Japanese: このプログラムに、数億円の 価値がある、ということです！
English: A virus like "MC Bomber" would be worth several million dollars on the black market.

Japanese: す‥‥すうおくえん‥‥！
English: S-Several million dollars!?

Japanese: 返せるアテのない人間にカネを 貸すなど、本来はあり得ない。
English: Lending money with no hope of ever seeing repayment would normally be bad for business.

Japanese: この場合‥‥“返すアテがない” ことこそが、重要だったのです。
English: But in this case, the very fact that Glen Elg had no way to repay the money is crucial.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんは、 優秀なプログラマーでした。
English: Glen Elg was a programmer. A highly skilled programmer.

Japanese: この証人は、そのウデを 担保に、カネを貸したのです。
English: That skill was the collateral Mr. Elg put up in order to borrow the money.

Japanese: そのプログラムを手に入れる‥‥ それが《動機》だと言うつもりか？
English: You're trying to suggest the witness's motive was to get hold of that program?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: そこの証人は、バカじゃねえ。
English: The witness may have poor fashion sense, but he is by no means an idiot, Trite.

Japanese: 殺人なんかしなくても １億ぐらい、かせげただろうぜ。
English: A man like him could get his hands on one million dollars without resorting to murder.

Japanese: あるいは、そうかもしれません。 ‥‥時間さえあれば。
English: Of course he could... Provided that he had time.

Japanese: しかし‥‥どうしても、 その金が、“今”必要だとしたら？
English: But what if he had needed the money right then?

Japanese: 緊急事態で‥‥手段を選ぶ ヨユウがなかったとしたら‥‥？
English: When the pressure's on, the luxury of choice tends to disappear.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人には、何か 考えがあるようですね。
English: It seems you have a logical conclusion for this theory, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ここで、提示して いただきましょう。
English: Would you care to share it with us?

Japanese: この証人が、なぜ‥‥ 億単位のカネを必要としたのか？
English: Why did Mr. Tigre need money to the tune of one million dollars?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか、 この証拠品は。
English: What is the meaning of this, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: 弁護人が、裁判所に行ったことが ないことを示す証拠品だろうぜ。
English: It's evidence... that Trite has no idea what he's doing.

Japanese: な、何を言ってるんですか！ ぼくは、ここにこうして‥‥
English: What!? What do you mean?

Japanese: それだけ、マトはずれな 証拠だと言ってるんだ！
English: You're way off track, Trite. That evidence is irrelevant!

Japanese: （なんでコイツに ペナルティを食らうんだよ‥‥）
English: (Since when did he have the right to dish out penalties?)

Japanese: だ、だいじょうぶなの、 なるほどくん！
English: A-Are you alright, Nick?

Japanese: 芝九蔵は‥‥１０００万円じゃ 足りなかったんだよ。
English: 100,000 dollars wasn't enough for Mr. Tigre.

Japanese: どうしても、もっとカネが 必要だった！　緊急に‥‥。
English: He needed much more money than that. And in a hurry!

Japanese: 去年の１２月。あなたは多額の カネが、どうしても必要だった！
English: In December of last year, you found yourself in need of a huge amount of money.

Japanese: 今から半年ほど前‥‥ あなたは、交通事故を起こした。
English: About 6 months ago, you were involved in a traffic accident, weren't you?

Japanese: スクーターとクルマの接触事故。 ひとりの女性が、ケガを負った‥‥
English: An accident involving a car and a scooter, in which a young woman was injured.

Japanese: 女性は病院に運びこまれて、 手術を受けたそうです。
English: She was taken to the hospital, where she underwent surgery.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、アンタ‥‥ どこまで知っとるんやァ‥‥
English: Wh-Where did youse get all this info...?

Japanese: これが、そのときケガを負わせた 女性の診断書です。
English: These medical papers document the treatment of the young woman in question.

Japanese: 手術代として‥‥１億円が 請求されています。
English: According to these, her operation cost... one million dollars!

Japanese: 支払い期限は、去年の１２月中‥‥ どうやら、払えたようですね。
English: Payment for these expenses was due in December of last year, and was paid in full!

Japanese: い‥‥イチオクエン‥‥！ な、なんとバカげた‥‥
English: O-One million dollars!? A preposterous sum! Someone should sue these HMOs!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　そんな請求、 マジメに払うヤツはいねえぜ。
English: Ha...! No one would pay a bill like that.

Japanese: 医師会に持ちこめば、 その病院はアウト‥‥だ。
English: If the medical association got wind of it, the hospital would end up as dead as a morgue.

Japanese: その金額がいくらであろうと、 彼は支払うしかなかった！
English: But Mr. Tigre had no choice but to pay!

Japanese: なにしろ、自分の命が かかっているのですからね！
English: Because his very life depended on it!

Japanese: ぐわおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Grrraaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ い‥‥“命”というのは‥‥！
English: O-Order! Order! Order! You say his life depended on it, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: 《ケガをさせた女性》‥‥それが、 鹿羽　うらみさんだったのです。
English: Indeed it did. Simply because the injured woman was none other than Viola Cadaverini.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ “シカバネ”‥‥だと？
English: Did you say, "Cadaverini"?

Japanese: この街の闇金融のボス、 《鹿羽組》組長・鹿羽　権太。
English: Bruto Cadaverini. Mob boss in charge of all underworld activities in the city, and...

Japanese: 鹿羽　うらみさんは、 そのマゴ娘です。
English: ...doting grandfather to his precious "Violetta", also known as Viola Cadaverini.

Japanese: ぐふおおおおおおッ！
English: Bwooooar!

Japanese: “オトシマエ”をつけなければ、 命があぶない。‥‥だからこそ！
English: Your life was in danger unless you paid "compensation" to the boss, correct? It makes sense!

Japanese: あなたは、なんとしても １億円を用意するしかなかった！
English: You were desperate to acquire the one million dollars Cadaverini demanded of you!

Japanese: そのために、岡　高夫さんの 命をギセイにしたのです！
English: So desperate, in fact, that you decided to sacrifice Glen Elg's life to pay your debt!

Japanese: あの日！　芝九蔵さんは、この ＣＤを手に入れるつもりでした！
English: On the day of the murder, Mr. Tigre's sole intention was to get his hands on this CD!

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんに、１０００万円を 用意できるわけがない。
English: Glen Elg had no way of paying back the 100,000 dollars, and Mr. Tigre knew it.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！　そこで、 《奇跡》が起こってしまった！
English: But then a miracle happened.

Japanese: ‥‥決して起こっては ならない、《奇跡》が‥‥！
English: The kind that Mr. Tigre would prefer to say never happened. But he can't. Neither can I.

Japanese: ‥‥宝クジ‥‥か！
English: The lottery win?

Japanese: 最後の最後‥‥岡　高夫さんは ５０００万円を当ててしまった！
English: Exactly! At the eleventh hour, Mr. Elg won half a million dollars on the lottery!

Japanese: これで‥‥芝九蔵さんは、ＣＤを 手に入れることはできなくなった。
English: Which left Mr. Tigre with no way of getting his hands on the all-important CD.

Japanese: フツウの手段では‥‥。
English: At least, no legitimate way.

Japanese: ‥‥だから。 フツウじゃない手段を使った！
English: So he resorted to illegitimate means.

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな！
English: That's crazy!

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんを殺害して‥‥ さらに犯人は、次の手を打った。
English: He murdered Glen Elg, and then made his next move...

Japanese: ‥‥そのツミを、 須々木　マコに着せる工作です。
English: To frame Maggey Byrde for the crime!

Japanese: 岡　高夫になりすました 芝九蔵　虎ノ助と、
English: Mr. Tigre posed as Glen Elg...

Japanese: 須々木　マコを演じた 鹿羽　うらみ‥‥。
English: ...while Viola Cadaverini played the role of Ms. Byrde.

Japanese: ２人は、犯行を再現した！ ‥‥《目撃者》を作るために。
English: And then they re-enacted the whole thing, in order to establish a witness.

Japanese: その《目撃者》こそが、 五十嵐　将兵さんだったのです！
English: The witness being the one we all heard testify yesterday, Mr. Victor Kudo.

Japanese: バカな！　そんな大がかりな シカケ‥‥不可能に決まっている！
English: Like I said, Trite, that's crazy. No one could pull off a stunt like that.

Japanese: どうしたって、あのシェフに 気づかれちまっただろうぜ！
English: For starters, there's no way the chef could've been kept out of it.

Japanese: もちろん、本土坊さんは 計画を知っていたのです！
English: But Mr. Armstrong knew all about it.

Japanese: ‥‥お忘れですか？
English: Have you forgotten already, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: 店長の本土坊さんは、証人に ５０００万円の借金があった。
English: Mr. Armstrong owed the witness money, too. Half a million dollars, in fact.

Japanese: だから！　芝九蔵　虎ノ助に 協力するしかなかったのです！
English: He had no choice but to go along with Mr. Tigre's plan!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にぃッ！ 静まらなければ、退廷を‥‥
English: Order! Order! Silence, or I will clear the courtroom!

Japanese: ぐわーっはっはっはっはっはっは！
English: Grrraah ha ha ha ha hah!

Japanese: ‥‥アホもここまで来れば リッパなゲージュツやのぉ‥‥
English: Youse put on a good show, Spikey!

Japanese: 金貸しはなァ。用心深くなきゃ 生きていけへんショーバイや。
English: If youse wanna stay alive in the loan shark business, you' gotta be careful.

Japanese: 被害者に変装して、目撃者を作って ツミを着せる工作をした、やてェ？
English: Youse sayin' I dressed up like that kid, created a witness, and framed someone?

Japanese: そないなコトしたら、 よけいなショーコを残すだけや！
English: If I did something crazy like that, I'd leave a trail as bright as my shirt!

Japanese: そんな中途ハンパなコト、 ワイはせえへんでェ！
English: I ain't dumb enough to do something sloppy like that!

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: I agree.

Japanese: な‥‥なんや‥‥ッ！
English: Whaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは見かけによらず、 用心深い。
English: Despite your appearance, you are very careful.

Japanese: だから‥‥さらに もう１つ、用意したのです。
English: That's why you took one more precaution.

Japanese: 須々木　マコを、カクジツに 有罪にするためのワナをッ！
English: One more trick to make sure Ms. Byrde had no way out!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！ まだ、ワナがあったのですか‥‥！
English: What!? Another one, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥おもしろい。 見せてもらおうじゃねえか‥‥
English: Interesting. Why don't you fill us all in, Trite?

Japanese: 芝九蔵　虎ノ助が被告人にしかけた もう１つのワナ、ってヤツを！
English: What was this trick you say Mr. Tigre performed to frame the accused?

Japanese: ‥‥で？　今のコレは、なんだ。 まるほどう‥‥？
English: And? What's the relevance of that, Trite?

Japanese: え！　あの‥‥ ちょっとした、アイサツです。
English: Um... It's, uh... just a little intro...

Japanese: ‥‥まったく、 とんだごアイサツ、だぜ。
English: An intro into what?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥すべては、この 弁護人のワナだったようですな。
English: It seems all this has been an elaborate trick on the part of the defense.

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: No! Wait!

Japanese: 芝九蔵　虎ノ助のワナは、 たしかに存在したのです！
English: Mr. Tigre did play a trick! A huge trick!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、須々木　マコに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maggey Byrde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
